DEFENDANTS GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC., AND LI CAO'S Motion For An Order Establishing Admissions Or Compelling Responses And Further Responses, And Compelling Responses And Further Responses To Their First Set Of Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions:

Motion granted in part, denied in part. The Court finds based on the LaLanne Declaration, and not contradicted by the Sarkar declaration, that the written discovery was hand served on February 25, but was apparently left on the back deck of attorney LaLanne's office. It is debatable under CCP sec. 1011(a) whether this is proper personal service, in that it was not left with a person, and was left on the back deck. Because of how the discovery was served, it was a foreseeable and inadvertent mistake by attorney LaLanne to not realize that the discovery had been hand served and to calendar responses as if due by mail. The Court further finds that attorney LaLanne's proof of service of responses by mail is valid and that objections were not waived.

The Court also finds that moving party rushed this motion and failed to engage in a substantive meet and confer effort. Moving party ignored the well-known rule that, even where responses to Request for Admissions are late, as long as responses in "substantial compliance" with CCP 2033.220 are served prior to the hearing, the motion to establish admissions is defeated. No showing of "mistake, inadvertence or excusable neglect" is required. [Tobin v. Oris, supra, 3 CA4th at 828) The motion is therefore DENIED as to the RFAs.

The Court finds that the objections to Special Interrogatories (on grounds that the interrogatories are not complete in and of themselves) are not well-stated and overrules the objections. The interrogatories are reasonably clear and the responding parties should have made an effort to provide a good faith response. The motion to compel responses is GRANTED as to all Special Interrogatories. The Court orders San Dong Best Inc. and Shubo Wang to provide complete, verified, objection-free responses within 14 days of service of this Order.

The Court sustains the objections to Demand for Production No. 6 and 18, as these requests are extremely overboard and compound, and invade financial privacy. No further response is required to those requests. To the extent San Dong Best Inc. and Shubo Wang still owe documents they said they would produce in their responses to Demand For Production, the Court orders production within 14 days of service of this Order.

With regard to form interrogatories, San Dong Best Inc. and Shubo Wang shall within 14 days provide responses to all form interrogatories to which they did not respond.

Sanctions denied as to both sides. In particular, with regard to RFAs, no sanctions of any kind can be imposed for mere delay in responding to RFAs if no motion has yet been filed. (St. Mary v. Superior Court (2014) 223 CA4th 762, 784.) The motion to have matters deemed admitted was thus a non-starter before it was even filed. The Court finds that both sides made mistakes in this discovery dispute, and this will not be rewarded with sanctions.

Order is signed in open court.
